articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Clean Air' as Fundamental Right, Mandates State Action
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court declared the 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of the Right to Life under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling mandates central and state governments to formulate and implement stringent policies for air quality improvement and pollution control.
• The Court emphasized the need for a rights-based approach to environmental protection, holding the state accountable for ensuring a healthy environment.
• A new national task force, with judicial oversight, has been proposed to monitor compliance and progress on air pollution mitigation.
• This decision is expected to significantly strengthen environmental jurisprudence and public health advocacy in India.
gsSubTopics: Fundamental Rights
Judiciary
Environmental Protection Acts
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
The 'Right to Clean Air' has been interpreted by the Supreme Court as implicit within Article 21.
The judgment emphasizes the role of the state in ensuring a pollution-free environment.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) is a statutory body for environmental cases.
Environmental Protection Act, 1986 provides for the protection and improvement of the environment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of declaring 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right on government policy formulation and implementation.
Analyze the role of judicial activism in expanding the scope of fundamental rights and ensuring environmental justice.
Examine the challenges in enforcing environmental regulations and ensuring compliance across various sectors in India.
Critically evaluate the balance between economic development and environmental protection in the context of this judgment.
Suggest policy measures and institutional reforms required to effectively address air pollution in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block with a backdrop of a clear sky and clean environment, symbolizing justice for environmental rights.
articles.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality a critical public health issue.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Phase
articles.explainer:
• Talks between India and the European Union for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have progressed to a critical stage, with both sides expressing optimism.
• Key areas of discussion include market access for goods and services, intellectual property rights, investment protection, and geographical indications.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially transforming India's economic relations with the 27-member bloc.
• Challenges remain, particularly concerning tariffs on sensitive agricultural products and non-tariff barriers related to regulatory standards.
• A breakthrough is anticipated in the coming months, which could position India as a major global trade partner for the EU.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India
Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
FTA aims to reduce trade barriers like tariffs and quotas between signatory countries.
India's major exports to EU include textiles, chemicals, agricultural products, and machinery.
India's major imports from EU include machinery, transport equipment, and chemicals.
Geographical Indications (GIs) protect products specific to a geographical origin.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges for India from a comprehensive FTA with the European Union.
Discuss how an India-EU FTA could reshape global trade dynamics and India's position in the international economic order.
Examine the critical areas of disagreement in the ongoing negotiations and possible compromises from both sides.
Evaluate the implications of such an agreement on specific Indian sectors like agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing.
Critically assess the role of trade agreements in India's 'Act East' and 'Look West' policies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union shaking hands, symbolizing cooperation and trade.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for over 10% of India's total trade in goods.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches EOS-07, Bolstering Earth Observation Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including urban planning, agriculture, forestry, and disaster management.
• The satellite was launched using the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), marking another successful mission for the reliable workhorse rocket.
• This mission further enhances India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to global efforts in climate monitoring.
• Data from EOS-07 will be crucial for governmental agencies and researchers in resource mapping and environmental assessment.
gsSubTopics: Space Technology
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) SHAR, Sriharikota, is India's primary spaceport.
Earth observation satellites are used for monitoring land, ocean, and atmospheric phenomena.
Applications include weather forecasting, mapping, and environmental studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's national development and strategic interests.
Evaluate ISRO's role in advancing India's space capabilities and its contributions to global space exploration.
Analyze the various applications of remote sensing technology in sectors such as agriculture, urban planning, and disaster management.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the global space industry, including commercialization and international collaborations.
How does indigenous space technology contribute to India's self-reliance (Aatmanirbhar Bharat) vision?
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with the ISRO logo visible, against a backdrop of Earth.
articles.trivia: India operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

articles.headline: PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 Launched to Enhance Digital Governance in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj launched PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0, an upgraded version of the e-governance platform for rural local bodies.
• The initiative aims to digitalize all aspects of Gram Panchayat functioning, from planning and budgeting to accounting and service delivery.
• It integrates various rural development schemes and services, providing a single window for citizens to access information and lodge grievances.
• PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 emphasizes transparency, accountability, and efficiency in local governance, aligning with the Digital India mission.
• Training and capacity building programs for Panchayat members and officials are a key component of the rollout to ensure effective adoption.
gsSubTopics: E-governance
Panchayati Raj
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Digital India
prelimsPoints: PM-eGram Swaraj is an e-governance application for Panchayati Raj Institutions.
The Ministry of Panchayati Raj is the nodal ministry for this initiative.
The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act institutionalized Panchayati Raj.
Digital India aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
The platform covers planning, accounting, and monitoring functions of Gram Panchayats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 on transparency, accountability, and efficiency in rural local governance.
Discuss the challenges in implementing e-governance initiatives in rural areas, particularly concerning digital literacy and infrastructure.
Evaluate how digital platforms like PM-eGram Swaraj contribute to the empowerment of Panchayati Raj Institutions and citizen participation.
Examine the role of technology in bridging the urban-rural divide in public service delivery and governance.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of digital governance tools by all sections of rural society.
articles.imageDescription: A digital map of rural India with interconnected nodes, symbolizing e-governance and connectivity.
articles.trivia: India has over 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats, serving a significant portion of its population.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India, along with other G4 nations, intensified its advocacy for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The discussions centered on expanding both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India emphasized that the current composition of the UNSC does not adequately represent the global South or the world's most populous democracy.
• The reform push aims to make the UNSC more democratic, representative, and effective in addressing 21st-century challenges.
• Despite broad support, the reform process faces significant hurdles from existing permanent members and regional rivalries.
gsSubTopics: Important International Institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: The G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, and Japan) advocate for permanent seats on the UNSC.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) with veto power and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India has been elected as a non-permanent member of the UNSC multiple times.
The UN Charter outlines the structure and functions of the Security Council.
The primary responsibility of the UNSC is the maintenance of international peace and security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UN Security Council's permanent membership.
Analyze why India seeks a permanent seat in the UNSC and what challenges it faces in achieving this objective.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the current UNSC structure in addressing contemporary global conflicts and humanitarian crises.
Examine the implications of a reformed UNSC for global governance and the balance of power among nations.
What role do regional blocs and groupings play in advocating for or opposing UNSC reforms?
articles.imageDescription: A collage of flags of G4 nations superimposed on the United Nations logo, symbolizing the demand for reform.
articles.trivia: The last significant reform of the UNSC occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

articles.headline: National Water Policy 2025 Framework Prioritizes Integrated Water Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti unveiled the draft framework for the National Water Policy 2025, focusing on integrated water resource management.
• The policy emphasizes a shift from a sectoral approach to a holistic, basin-level strategy, promoting inter-state cooperation.
• Key pillars include water conservation, efficient use, groundwater recharge, and sustainable management of both surface and groundwater resources.
• It advocates for the 'water-food-energy nexus' approach, recognizing the interdependence of these critical sectors.
• Public participation, data-driven decision making, and adoption of modern technologies are also central to the new policy's vision.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water Resources
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry for water resources in India.
Integrated Water Resource Management (IWRM) is a process which promotes the coordinated development and management of water, land and related resources.
National Water Policy (NWP) guides the planning and development of water resources.
Groundwater is a significant source of irrigation and drinking water in India.
The policy aims to address issues like water scarcity and pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Water Policy 2025 and its potential to address India's water crisis.
Discuss the challenges in implementing an integrated water resource management approach, particularly concerning inter-state water disputes and federalism.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous water policies in India and how the new framework aims to overcome their limitations.
Examine the importance of the 'water-food-energy nexus' in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
Suggest innovative solutions and community-based approaches for water conservation and efficient water use in both urban and rural areas.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a river flowing into a reservoir, with elements of agriculture and energy production, symbolizing integrated water management.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

articles.headline: Comprehensive Cybersecurity Policy 2025 Unveiled Amid Rising Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025, replacing previous guidelines.
• The policy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and critical national infrastructure.
• Key focus areas include strengthening cyber defense capabilities, promoting indigenous cybersecurity products, and fostering international cooperation.
• It mandates robust security frameworks for government agencies and critical sectors, along with a focus on skill development and public awareness.
• The new framework addresses emerging threats like AI-powered cyberattacks, ransomware, and supply chain vulnerabilities.
gsSubTopics: Cyber Security
Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks
Role of Media and Social Networking Sites in Internal Security Challenges
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is the nodal ministry for cybersecurity.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to cyber incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy.
The policy aims to protect against cyberattacks, data breaches, and digital espionage.
India's first National Cyber Security Policy was released in 2013.
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025 and how it addresses contemporary cyber threats.
Analyze the challenges India faces in securing its cyberspace, considering its vast digital footprint and evolving threat landscape.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and collaboration in building a robust global cybersecurity framework.
Examine the interplay between cybersecurity, data privacy, and national security in the digital age.
Suggest measures to enhance public awareness and develop a skilled cybersecurity workforce in India.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock icon superimposed on a digital circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity and protection.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Progresses
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting achievements in foundational learning.
• Significant strides have been made in strengthening foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) through initiatives like NIPUN Bharat Mission.
• The report details increased teacher training, development of new curriculum frameworks, and integration of technology in early childhood education.
• Challenges persist in ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in remote and socio-economically disadvantaged regions.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands, focusing on holistic development and critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
NIPUN Bharat Mission aims to ensure foundational literacy and numeracy for all children by 2026-27.
The Ministry of Education is responsible for implementing NEP 2020.
NEP 2020 advocates for a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
The policy emphasizes multilingualism and the use of technology in education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms introduced by NEP 2020, with a special focus on foundational literacy and numeracy.
Discuss the progress and challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in achieving equitable educational outcomes across regions.
Evaluate the role of teacher training and curriculum development in enhancing the quality of early childhood education in India.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to prepare Indian students for the demands of the 21st-century job market.
Suggest innovative strategies to overcome the digital divide and ensure inclusive access to online learning resources in the context of NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Children in a classroom learning with interactive tools, symbolizing foundational education.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amid Persistent Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was driven by persistent concerns over elevated inflation, despite recent moderation in some indicators.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target range of 2-6%.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while prioritizing price stability.
• Market analysts anticipate a possible rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflationary trends and robust economic data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Reverse Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of fostering economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation and ensuring financial stability in India.
Examine the factors contributing to persistent inflation in India and the government's role in addressing supply-side constraints.
How do global economic conditions and central bank actions in other major economies influence RBI's monetary policy decisions?
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing a stable repo rate line amidst fluctuating inflation indicators, symbolizing RBI's policy stance.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Battle of Plassey Anniversary: Reassessing Colonial Legacy and Economic Drain
articles.explainer:
• The upcoming anniversary of the Battle of Plassey (June 23, 1757) prompts renewed discussions among historians and scholars on its long-term impact.
• The battle marked a pivotal moment, effectively establishing the East India Company's political dominance in Bengal and paving the way for British rule in India.
• Scholars are re-evaluating the 'economic drain' theory, examining how Bengal's wealth was systematically transferred to Britain after Plassey.
• The event highlights the complex interplay of political betrayal, military strategy, and economic exploitation that characterized early colonial expansion.
• Contemporary discussions emphasize the need to understand this historical turning point in the context of India's struggle for economic sovereignty.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian History from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
British Conquest and Consolidation
prelimsPoints: The Battle of Plassey was fought on June 23, 1757.
It was fought between the forces of the British East India Company led by Robert Clive and the Nawab of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daulah.
Mir Jafar, Siraj-ud-Daulah's commander-in-chief, betrayed the Nawab.
The battle led to the establishment of British political supremacy in Bengal.
Dadabhai Naoroji propounded the 'Drain of Wealth' theory.
mainsPoints: Analyze the political and economic significance of the Battle of Plassey in the context of the British conquest of India.
Discuss the 'economic drain' theory and how the Battle of Plassey facilitated the transfer of wealth from India to Britain.
Evaluate the role of Indian collaborators and internal dissensions in the rise of British power in the 18th century.
Examine the long-term socio-economic consequences of early colonial rule in Bengal following the Battle of Plassey.
Critically assess how different historical perspectives interpret the Battle of Plassey and its legacy.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic depiction of the Battle of Plassey, showing British and Indian forces engaged in conflict.
articles.trivia: The Battle of Plassey was relatively short, lasting only a few hours, largely due to the betrayal of Mir Jafar.

articles.headline: Deliberations on Uniform Civil Code Intensify in Parliamentary Panel
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee is actively deliberating on the feasibility and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for India.
• The discussions involve legal experts, religious leaders, and civil society representatives, examining various personal laws.
• Proponents argue UCC promotes gender equality and national integration, aligning with Article 44 of the Constitution.
• Opponents raise concerns about potential infringement on religious freedom and cultural diversity, advocating for reforms within existing personal laws.
• The committee's report is highly anticipated, as it could shape future policy decisions on this contentious constitutional matter.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Directive Principles of State Policy
Social Empowerment
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy that states 'The State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.'
Personal laws (e.g., Hindu Marriage Act, Muslim Personal Law) govern matters like marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Shah Bano case (1985) and Sarla Mudgal case (1995) are significant judgments related to UCC.
A parliamentary standing committee examines legislative and policy matters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context surrounding the demand for a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, religious freedom, and national integration.
Examine the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC in a diverse country like India, with multiple religious and cultural practices.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in advocating for a UCC and its limitations in compelling legislative action.
Suggest a balanced approach to personal law reforms that respects religious diversity while upholding principles of equality and justice.
articles.imageDescription: A balance scale weighing religious symbols against a law book, symbolizing the debate around UCC.
articles.trivia: The idea of a Uniform Civil Code dates back to the colonial era, initially proposed in the Lex Loci Report of 1840.

articles.headline: Project Great Indian Bustard Sees Boost with New Conservation Zones
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the establishment of new dedicated conservation zones for the critically endangered Great Indian Bustard (GIB).
• These zones, primarily in Rajasthan and Gujarat, aim to protect GIB habitats from human encroachment and infrastructure development, particularly power lines.
• The initiative includes captive breeding programs, grassland restoration, and community engagement to ensure the long-term survival of the species.
• The GIB population has seen a significant decline due to habitat loss, poaching, and collision with power transmission lines.
• This renewed focus is part of India's broader commitment to biodiversity conservation and protecting its unique fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental Impact Assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is a critically endangered bird species.
Its primary habitats are grasslands in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
GIB is listed in Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
Project GIB is a conservation initiative for the species.
Collision with overhead power lines is a major threat to GIB.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Great Indian Bustard and the factors contributing to its critically endangered status.
Analyze the measures being undertaken under Project GIB and other conservation initiatives to protect the species.
Examine the challenges in implementing wildlife conservation efforts, particularly concerning infrastructure development and human-wildlife conflict.
Evaluate the role of community participation and indigenous knowledge in successful conservation programs for endangered species.
Suggest policy interventions and technological solutions to mitigate threats like power line collisions and habitat fragmentation for the GIB.
articles.imageDescription: A Great Indian Bustard walking in its natural grassland habitat, with a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The Great Indian Bustard is the state bird of Rajasthan.

articles.headline: India's Indigenous 6G Technology Development Gains Momentum, Targets 2030 Rollout
articles.explainer:
• India's 6G Technology Mission, launched earlier, is accelerating research and development efforts, aiming for a commercial rollout by 2030.
• The mission focuses on developing indigenous capabilities across all aspects of 6G, including spectrum allocation, network architecture, and device manufacturing.
• Key research areas include AI integration, holographic communication, quantum computing applications, and sustainable energy-efficient networks.
• This push for indigenous 6G is crucial for India's strategic autonomy in telecommunications and to avoid reliance on foreign technologies.
• Collaborative efforts between academia, industry, and government are driving innovation and intellectual property creation in this advanced tech domain.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Indigenization of Technology
prelimsPoints: 6G is the sixth generation of wireless technology, succeeding 5G.
India's 6G Vision Document outlines the roadmap for development.
Key features of 6G are expected to include ultra-low latency, high bandwidth, and AI integration.
The mission aims for indigenous development and intellectual property creation.
Telecommunications Engineering Centre (TEC) is involved in standardization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing indigenous 6G technology for India's economic growth and national security.
Analyze the potential applications and transformative impact of 6G technology across various sectors, from healthcare to manufacturing.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a robust 6G ecosystem in India, including infrastructure, skill development, and research funding.
Evaluate India's position in the global race for 6G technology and the role of international collaborations.
How can policy support and regulatory frameworks accelerate the adoption and equitable access of advanced communication technologies like 6G?
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic network of interconnected devices and holographic projections, symbolizing 6G capabilities.
articles.trivia: While 5G is still being rolled out globally, research into 6G began around 2020.

articles.headline: Quad Leaders Hold Virtual Summit, Reaffirm Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• Leaders from the Quad group (India, Australia, Japan, and the United States) held a virtual summit to discuss regional and global challenges.
• The summit reaffirmed their shared commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law.
• Discussions focused on maritime security, critical and emerging technologies, climate change cooperation, and vaccine diplomacy.
• The leaders stressed the importance of strengthening supply chain resilience and addressing economic coercion in the region.
• This virtual engagement underscores the Quad's evolving role as a key platform for multilateral cooperation and regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) members are India, Australia, Japan, and the United States.
The concept of 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' is central to Quad's agenda.
Quad operates on the principle of a rules-based international order.
Areas of cooperation include maritime security, critical technologies, and climate change.
Malabar naval exercise involves all four Quad member countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and significance of the Quad in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Analyze the key areas of cooperation within the Quad framework and their implications for regional security and economic stability.
Evaluate the challenges and criticisms faced by the Quad, particularly regarding perceptions of it being an 'Asian NATO'.
Examine India's strategic interests and role within the Quad, balancing its relationships with other major powers.
How does the Quad contribute to strengthening supply chain resilience and promoting critical and emerging technologies in the Indo-Pacific?
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India, Australia, Japan, and USA forming a circle, symbolizing the Quad alliance.
articles.trivia: The Quad was first conceptualized in 2007 but was revived in 2017.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Green Hydrogen and Advanced Batteries Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include Green Hydrogen and Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Batteries.
• This move aims to boost domestic manufacturing, reduce import dependence, and position India as a global hub for these future technologies.
• The scheme offers incentives to companies for incremental sales from products manufactured in India, promoting scale and efficiency.
• The expansion is critical for India's energy transition goals, fostering the adoption of clean energy and electric mobility.
• It is expected to attract significant investments, create jobs, and enhance India's competitiveness in global green technology markets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Infrastructure: Energy
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme provides incentives to companies on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries are critical for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependence on key components and technologies.
Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan (Self-Reliant India Campaign) focuses on boosting domestic production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind expanding the PLI scheme to Green Hydrogen and Advanced Batteries and its significance for India's economic and environmental goals.
Analyze how the PLI scheme contributes to the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' vision by promoting domestic manufacturing and reducing import dependence.
Evaluate the potential impact of these incentives on the growth of India's renewable energy sector and electric vehicle ecosystem.
Examine the challenges that India might face in becoming a global hub for Green Hydrogen and ACC battery manufacturing.
Suggest complementary policy measures that can further accelerate investment and innovation in these emerging sectors.
articles.imageDescription: A factory silhouette with solar panels and wind turbines, and icons for hydrogen and batteries, symbolizing green manufacturing.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.

articles.headline: Mental Health Awareness Campaign 2.0 Launched with Focus on Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched 'Manas Sampark 2.0', a nationwide mental health awareness campaign, targeting youth and adolescents.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental health issues, promote early intervention, and provide accessible support systems.
• It integrates digital platforms, school-based programs, and community outreach to reach a wider audience and foster open conversations.
• Special emphasis is placed on addressing stress, anxiety, and depression among students, exacerbated by academic pressures and social media.
• This initiative builds upon the National Mental Health Program, reinforcing India's commitment to holistic well-being.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Program (NMHP) was launched in 1982.
The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, ensures rights-based mental healthcare.
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is the nodal ministry.
Youth and adolescents are a key demographic for mental health initiatives.
Tele-MANAS is a national tele-mental health program in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of mental health issues among youth in India and the factors contributing to it.
Analyze the objectives and strategies of 'Manas Sampark 2.0' in promoting mental health awareness and destigmatization.
Evaluate the gaps in India's mental healthcare infrastructure and policy, particularly in reaching vulnerable populations.
Examine the role of digital platforms and community-based interventions in providing accessible mental health support.
Suggest multi-sectoral approaches, involving education, family, and media, to foster a supportive environment for mental well-being.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse young people engaged in a supportive conversation, with a thought bubble icon above.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians are affected by mental health disorders to varying degrees.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Embraces Digital Promotion to Attract Global Travelers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched an aggressive digital marketing campaign, 'Incredible India 2.0', targeting international tourists.
• The campaign leverages AI-driven personalized travel itineraries, virtual reality tours of heritage sites, and social media influencers.
• Focus is on promoting niche tourism segments such as eco-tourism, adventure tourism, medical tourism, and spiritual tourism.
• The initiative aims to revive the tourism sector post-pandemic, enhance India's global image, and boost foreign exchange earnings.
• Collaboration with travel aggregators and airlines is a key strategy to offer seamless travel experiences and attract diverse demographics.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Infrastructure: Tourism
Impact of Globalization on Indian Society
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism is responsible for the promotion of tourism in India.
'Incredible India' is an international tourism campaign by the Indian government.
Medical tourism involves people traveling to other countries for medical treatment.
Eco-tourism focuses on responsible travel to natural areas.
Foreign exchange earnings from tourism contribute significantly to India's economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of the tourism sector for India's economic growth and employment generation.
Analyze how digital promotion strategies like 'Incredible India 2.0' can enhance India's global tourism footprint.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments such as eco-tourism, medical tourism, and spiritual tourism in attracting diverse travelers.
Examine the challenges faced by India's tourism sector, including infrastructure gaps, safety concerns, and environmental sustainability.
Suggest policy measures and public-private partnerships to foster sustainable and inclusive tourism development in India.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian landmarks (Taj Mahal, Himalayas, Kerala backwaters) with digital elements overlaid.
articles.trivia: India's tourism sector contributed 5.8% to the country's GDP in 2023.

articles.headline: Agri-Tech Startups Revolutionizing Indian Agriculture, Boosting Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• A new report highlights the rapid growth of Agri-Tech startups in India, leveraging technology to address agricultural challenges.
• These startups offer innovative solutions such as precision farming, supply chain optimization, market linkages, and access to credit.
• The integration of AI, IoT, and data analytics is enabling farmers to make informed decisions, reduce waste, and improve productivity.
• Government support through initiatives like Agri-Startup Policy and incubation centers is fostering a vibrant ecosystem for agricultural innovation.
• The rise of Agri-Tech is crucial for enhancing farmer income, ensuring food security, and modernizing India's agricultural sector.
gsSubTopics: Major Crops - Cropping Patterns in various parts of the country, - Different Types of Irrigation and Irrigation Systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Technology Missions
Economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Agri-Tech refers to the use of technology in agriculture.
Precision farming involves using data to optimize agricultural inputs.
Government promotes agri-startups through schemes like Agri-Startup Policy.
AI and IoT are being used for crop monitoring, soil analysis, and weather forecasting.
E-NAM (National Agriculture Market) is an online trading platform for agricultural commodities.
mainsPoints: Discuss how Agri-Tech startups are transforming the Indian agricultural landscape and contributing to farmer welfare.
Analyze the role of technology (AI, IoT, data analytics) in addressing key challenges faced by Indian farmers, such as productivity and market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and initiatives in fostering a conducive environment for agricultural innovation and startups.
Examine the potential of Agri-Tech to ensure food security, reduce post-harvest losses, and promote sustainable farming practices.
Suggest strategies to bridge the digital divide in rural areas and ensure that small and marginal farmers can effectively utilize Agri-Tech solutions.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet in a field, with drones and smart irrigation systems in the background, symbolizing Agri-Tech.
articles.trivia: India has emerged as the third-largest Agri-Tech ecosystem globally, after the US and China.

highlights: Supreme Court declares 'Right to Clean Air' a fundamental right under Article 21, mandating stricter environmental policies.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations enter a crucial phase, aiming to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment.
ISRO successfully launches Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07), enhancing India's remote sensing capabilities for various applications.
PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 launched to digitalize rural governance, promoting transparency and efficiency in Panchayati Raj Institutions.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate, citing persistent inflation concerns, while reiterating commitment to growth and price stability.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment on 'Right to Clean Air', consider the following statements:
1. The Supreme Court declared 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. The judgment mandates only state governments to formulate and implement policies for air quality improvement.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Supreme Court declared 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of the Right to Life under Article 21, not Article 19. Statement 2 is incorrect: The judgment mandates both central and state governments to formulate and implement stringent policies for air quality improvement and pollution control.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies determines the policy interest rates (like repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth. It consists of six members, three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is/are correct?
1. It is listed as 'Critically Endangered' by the IUCN Red List.
2. Its primary habitats are dense forests in Eastern India.
3. It is listed in Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is indeed listed as 'Critically Endangered' by the IUCN Red List. Statement 2 is incorrect: Its primary habitats are grasslands and arid regions, mainly in Rajasthan and Gujarat, not dense forests in Eastern India. Statement 3 is correct: The GIB is listed in Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, affording it the highest level of protection.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the 'Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme':
1. It aims to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence.
2. It provides incentives to companies for incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
3. It has recently been expanded to include sectors like Green Hydrogen and Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Batteries.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The PLI scheme is designed to boost domestic manufacturing, reduce import dependence, and offers incentives on incremental sales of domestically manufactured goods. Its recent expansion to Green Hydrogen and ACC Batteries aligns with India's energy transition and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' goals.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, which of the following statements is correct?
options: (a) Article 32 of the Constitution mandates the State to secure a UCC.
(b) Goa is the only Indian state currently having a Uniform Civil Code.
(c) The UCC aims to unify all customary tribal laws across India.
(d) The Supreme Court has consistently ruled against the implementation of a UCC.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Goa is the only Indian state currently having a Uniform Civil Code.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Option (a) is incorrect; Article 44 (Directive Principle) mandates the State to secure a UCC, not Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies). Option (b) is correct; Goa is the only Indian state with a UCC, inherited from its Portuguese civil code. Option (c) is incorrect; while it aims for uniformity, the primary focus is on personal laws of major religious communities, not specifically all tribal customary laws. Option (d) is incorrect; the Supreme Court has on several occasions urged the government to implement a UCC.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's declaration of 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right marks a significant milestone in environmental jurisprudence. Critically analyze the implications of this judgment on environmental governance and the balance between development and ecological sustainability in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC judgment and its basis in Article 21. Discuss positive implications: stronger legal basis for environmental protection, increased state accountability, potential for citizen activism, and push for stricter policies. Analyze challenges: enforcement mechanisms, balancing economic development, inter-state cooperation, and capacity building. Conclude on the need for integrated approach and sustained political will.

mainsMocks.question: India's pursuit of a permanent seat in the UN Security Council reflects its growing global aspirations. Discuss the various arguments supporting India's claim for permanent membership and the major obstacles it faces in achieving this objective. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International Institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate. Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's aspiration for UNSC permanent seat. Present arguments for India's inclusion: democratic credentials, large population, economic power, contributions to UN peacekeeping, responsible nuclear power. Discuss obstacles: resistance from P5 members (especially China), lack of consensus among G4, regional rivalries, and the complex nature of UN reforms. Conclude on the need for sustained diplomatic efforts.

mainsMocks.question: The expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to sectors like Green Hydrogen and Advanced Batteries is a strategic move towards 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat'. Examine how this expansion can foster domestic manufacturing, reduce import dependence, and accelerate India's energy transition goals. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment. Infrastructure: Energy. Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the PLI scheme and its recent expansion. Explain how PLI works and its 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' objective. Detail how incentives for Green Hydrogen and ACC Batteries will boost domestic manufacturing (scale, technology transfer, R&D). Discuss reduction in import dependence (energy security, economic stability). Explain acceleration of energy transition (EV adoption, renewable energy storage). Highlight potential for job creation and export growth. Conclude with challenges and the need for a holistic policy framework.

quickBytes.text: The 'Right to Clean Air' has been declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
Goa is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code.
The Battle of Plassey, fought on June 23, 1757, marked the beginning of British political dominance in India.
The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is a critically endangered bird species, primarily found in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI determines the policy interest rates in India.
PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 aims to digitalize all aspects of Gram Panchayat functioning in India.
ISRO's PSLV is known as the 'workhorse' launch vehicle for its reliability and versatility.
The Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) comprises India, Australia, Japan, and the United States.
Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
NIPUN Bharat Mission is an initiative under NEP 2020 to ensure foundational literacy and numeracy.

GS1.headline: Battle of Plassey Anniversary: Reassessing Colonial Legacy and Economic Drain
GS1.explainer:
• The upcoming anniversary of the Battle of Plassey (June 23, 1757) prompts renewed discussions among historians and scholars on its long-term impact.
• The battle marked a pivotal moment, effectively establishing the East India Company's political dominance in Bengal and paving the way for British rule in India.
• Scholars are re-evaluating the 'economic drain' theory, examining how Bengal's wealth was systematically transferred to Britain after Plassey.
• The event highlights the complex interplay of political betrayal, military strategy, and economic exploitation that characterized early colonial expansion.
• Contemporary discussions emphasize the need to understand this historical turning point in the context of India's struggle for economic sovereignty.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian History from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
British Conquest and Consolidation
prelimsPoints: The Battle of Plassey was fought on June 23, 1757.
It was fought between the forces of the British East India Company led by Robert Clive and the Nawab of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daulah.
Mir Jafar, Siraj-ud-Daulah's commander-in-chief, betrayed the Nawab.
The battle led to the establishment of British political supremacy in Bengal.
Dadabhai Naoroji propounded the 'Drain of Wealth' theory.
mainsPoints: Analyze the political and economic significance of the Battle of Plassey in the context of the British conquest of India.
Discuss the 'economic drain' theory and how the Battle of Plassey facilitated the transfer of wealth from India to Britain.
Evaluate the role of Indian collaborators and internal dissensions in the rise of British power in the 18th century.
Examine the long-term socio-economic consequences of early colonial rule in Bengal following the Battle of Plassey.
Critically assess how different historical perspectives interpret the Battle of Plassey and its legacy.
GS1.imageDescription: An artistic depiction of the Battle of Plassey, showing British and Indian forces engaged in conflict.
GS1.trivia: The Battle of Plassey was relatively short, lasting only a few hours, largely due to the betrayal of Mir Jafar.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Clean Air' as Fundamental Right, Mandates State Action
GS2.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court declared the 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of the Right to Life under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling mandates central and state governments to formulate and implement stringent policies for air quality improvement and pollution control.
• The Court emphasized the need for a rights-based approach to environmental protection, holding the state accountable for ensuring a healthy environment.
• A new national task force, with judicial oversight, has been proposed to monitor compliance and progress on air pollution mitigation.
• This decision is expected to significantly strengthen environmental jurisprudence and public health advocacy in India.
gsSubTopics: Fundamental Rights
Judiciary
Environmental Protection Acts
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
The 'Right to Clean Air' has been interpreted by the Supreme Court as implicit within Article 21.
The judgment emphasizes the role of the state in ensuring a pollution-free environment.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) is a statutory body for environmental cases.
Environmental Protection Act, 1986 provides for the protection and improvement of the environment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of declaring 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right on government policy formulation and implementation.
Analyze the role of judicial activism in expanding the scope of fundamental rights and ensuring environmental justice.
Examine the challenges in enforcing environmental regulations and ensuring compliance across various sectors in India.
Critically evaluate the balance between economic development and environmental protection in the context of this judgment.
Suggest policy measures and institutional reforms required to effectively address air pollution in Indian cities.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block with a backdrop of a clear sky and clean environment, symbolizing justice for environmental rights.
GS2.trivia: India is home to many of the world's most polluted cities, making air quality a critical public health issue.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Phase
GS2.explainer:
• Talks between India and the European Union for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have progressed to a critical stage, with both sides expressing optimism.
• Key areas of discussion include market access for goods and services, intellectual property rights, investment protection, and geographical indications.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially transforming India's economic relations with the 27-member bloc.
• Challenges remain, particularly concerning tariffs on sensitive agricultural products and non-tariff barriers related to regulatory standards.
• A breakthrough is anticipated in the coming months, which could position India as a major global trade partner for the EU.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India
Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
FTA aims to reduce trade barriers like tariffs and quotas between signatory countries.
India's major exports to EU include textiles, chemicals, agricultural products, and machinery.
India's major imports from EU include machinery, transport equipment, and chemicals.
Geographical Indications (GIs) protect products specific to a geographical origin.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges for India from a comprehensive FTA with the European Union.
Discuss how an India-EU FTA could reshape global trade dynamics and India's position in the international economic order.
Examine the critical areas of disagreement in the ongoing negotiations and possible compromises from both sides.
Evaluate the implications of such an agreement on specific Indian sectors like agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing.
Critically assess the role of trade agreements in India's 'Act East' and 'Look West' policies.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union shaking hands, symbolizing cooperation and trade.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for over 10% of India's total trade in goods.

GS2.headline: PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 Launched to Enhance Digital Governance in Rural India
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj launched PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0, an upgraded version of the e-governance platform for rural local bodies.
• The initiative aims to digitalize all aspects of Gram Panchayat functioning, from planning and budgeting to accounting and service delivery.
• It integrates various rural development schemes and services, providing a single window for citizens to access information and lodge grievances.
• PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 emphasizes transparency, accountability, and efficiency in local governance, aligning with the Digital India mission.
• Training and capacity building programs for Panchayat members and officials are a key component of the rollout to ensure effective adoption.
gsSubTopics: E-governance
Panchayati Raj
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Digital India
prelimsPoints: PM-eGram Swaraj is an e-governance application for Panchayati Raj Institutions.
The Ministry of Panchayati Raj is the nodal ministry for this initiative.
The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act institutionalized Panchayati Raj.
Digital India aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
The platform covers planning, accounting, and monitoring functions of Gram Panchayats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of PM-eGram Swaraj 2.0 on transparency, accountability, and efficiency in rural local governance.
Discuss the challenges in implementing e-governance initiatives in rural areas, particularly concerning digital literacy and infrastructure.
Evaluate how digital platforms like PM-eGram Swaraj contribute to the empowerment of Panchayati Raj Institutions and citizen participation.
Examine the role of technology in bridging the urban-rural divide in public service delivery and governance.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of digital governance tools by all sections of rural society.
GS2.imageDescription: A digital map of rural India with interconnected nodes, symbolizing e-governance and connectivity.
GS2.trivia: India has over 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats, serving a significant portion of its population.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India, along with other G4 nations, intensified its advocacy for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The discussions centered on expanding both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India emphasized that the current composition of the UNSC does not adequately represent the global South or the world's most populous democracy.
• The reform push aims to make the UNSC more democratic, representative, and effective in addressing 21st-century challenges.
• Despite broad support, the reform process faces significant hurdles from existing permanent members and regional rivalries.
gsSubTopics: Important International Institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: The G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, and Japan) advocate for permanent seats on the UNSC.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) with veto power and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India has been elected as a non-permanent member of the UNSC multiple times.
The UN Charter outlines the structure and functions of the Security Council.
The primary responsibility of the UNSC is the maintenance of international peace and security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UN Security Council's permanent membership.
Analyze why India seeks a permanent seat in the UNSC and what challenges it faces in achieving this objective.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the current UNSC structure in addressing contemporary global conflicts and humanitarian crises.
Examine the implications of a reformed UNSC for global governance and the balance of power among nations.
What role do regional blocs and groupings play in advocating for or opposing UNSC reforms?
GS2.imageDescription: A collage of flags of G4 nations superimposed on the United Nations logo, symbolizing the demand for reform.
GS2.trivia: The last significant reform of the UNSC occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Progresses
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting achievements in foundational learning.
• Significant strides have been made in strengthening foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) through initiatives like NIPUN Bharat Mission.
• The report details increased teacher training, development of new curriculum frameworks, and integration of technology in early childhood education.
• Challenges persist in ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in remote and socio-economically disadvantaged regions.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands, focusing on holistic development and critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
NIPUN Bharat Mission aims to ensure foundational literacy and numeracy for all children by 2026-27.
The Ministry of Education is responsible for implementing NEP 2020.
NEP 2020 advocates for a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
The policy emphasizes multilingualism and the use of technology in education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms introduced by NEP 2020, with a special focus on foundational literacy and numeracy.
Discuss the progress and challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in achieving equitable educational outcomes across regions.
Evaluate the role of teacher training and curriculum development in enhancing the quality of early childhood education in India.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to prepare Indian students for the demands of the 21st-century job market.
Suggest innovative strategies to overcome the digital divide and ensure inclusive access to online learning resources in the context of NEP 2020.
GS2.imageDescription: Children in a classroom learning with interactive tools, symbolizing foundational education.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

GS2.headline: Deliberations on Uniform Civil Code Intensify in Parliamentary Panel
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee is actively deliberating on the feasibility and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for India.
• The discussions involve legal experts, religious leaders, and civil society representatives, examining various personal laws.
• Proponents argue UCC promotes gender equality and national integration, aligning with Article 44 of the Constitution.
• Opponents raise concerns about potential infringement on religious freedom and cultural diversity, advocating for reforms within existing personal laws.
• The committee's report is highly anticipated, as it could shape future policy decisions on this contentious constitutional matter.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Directive Principles of State Policy
Social Empowerment
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy that states 'The State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.'
Personal laws (e.g., Hindu Marriage Act, Muslim Personal Law) govern matters like marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Shah Bano case (1985) and Sarla Mudgal case (1995) are significant judgments related to UCC.
A parliamentary standing committee examines legislative and policy matters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context surrounding the demand for a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, religious freedom, and national integration.
Examine the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC in a diverse country like India, with multiple religious and cultural practices.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in advocating for a UCC and its limitations in compelling legislative action.
Suggest a balanced approach to personal law reforms that respects religious diversity while upholding principles of equality and justice.
GS2.imageDescription: A balance scale weighing religious symbols against a law book, symbolizing the debate around UCC.
GS2.trivia: The idea of a Uniform Civil Code dates back to the colonial era, initially proposed in the Lex Loci Report of 1840.

GS2.headline: Quad Leaders Hold Virtual Summit, Reaffirm Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• Leaders from the Quad group (India, Australia, Japan, and the United States) held a virtual summit to discuss regional and global challenges.
• The summit reaffirmed their shared commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law.
• Discussions focused on maritime security, critical and emerging technologies, climate change cooperation, and vaccine diplomacy.
• The leaders stressed the importance of strengthening supply chain resilience and addressing economic coercion in the region.
• This virtual engagement underscores the Quad's evolving role as a key platform for multilateral cooperation and regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) members are India, Australia, Japan, and the United States.
The concept of 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' is central to Quad's agenda.
Quad operates on the principle of a rules-based international order.
Areas of cooperation include maritime security, critical technologies, and climate change.
Malabar naval exercise involves all four Quad member countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and significance of the Quad in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Analyze the key areas of cooperation within the Quad framework and their implications for regional security and economic stability.
Evaluate the challenges and criticisms faced by the Quad, particularly regarding perceptions of it being an 'Asian NATO'.
Examine India's strategic interests and role within the Quad, balancing its relationships with other major powers.
How does the Quad contribute to strengthening supply chain resilience and promoting critical and emerging technologies in the Indo-Pacific?
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India, Australia, Japan, and USA forming a circle, symbolizing the Quad alliance.
GS2.trivia: The Quad was first conceptualized in 2007 but was revived in 2017.

GS2.headline: Mental Health Awareness Campaign 2.0 Launched with Focus on Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched 'Manas Sampark 2.0', a nationwide mental health awareness campaign, targeting youth and adolescents.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental health issues, promote early intervention, and provide accessible support systems.
• It integrates digital platforms, school-based programs, and community outreach to reach a wider audience and foster open conversations.
• Special emphasis is placed on addressing stress, anxiety, and depression among students, exacerbated by academic pressures and social media.
• This initiative builds upon the National Mental Health Program, reinforcing India's commitment to holistic well-being.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Program (NMHP) was launched in 1982.
The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, ensures rights-based mental healthcare.
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is the nodal ministry.
Youth and adolescents are a key demographic for mental health initiatives.
Tele-MANAS is a national tele-mental health program in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of mental health issues among youth in India and the factors contributing to it.
Analyze the objectives and strategies of 'Manas Sampark 2.0' in promoting mental health awareness and destigmatization.
Evaluate the gaps in India's mental healthcare infrastructure and policy, particularly in reaching vulnerable populations.
Examine the role of digital platforms and community-based interventions in providing accessible mental health support.
Suggest multi-sectoral approaches, involving education, family, and media, to foster a supportive environment for mental well-being.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of diverse young people engaged in a supportive conversation, with a thought bubble icon above.
GS2.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians are affected by mental health disorders to varying degrees.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches EOS-07, Bolstering Earth Observation Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including urban planning, agriculture, forestry, and disaster management.
• The satellite was launched using the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), marking another successful mission for the reliable workhorse rocket.
• This mission further enhances India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to global efforts in climate monitoring.
• Data from EOS-07 will be crucial for governmental agencies and researchers in resource mapping and environmental assessment.
gsSubTopics: Space Technology
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: EOS-07 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) SHAR, Sriharikota, is India's primary spaceport.
Earth observation satellites are used for monitoring land, ocean, and atmospheric phenomena.
Applications include weather forecasting, mapping, and environmental studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's national development and strategic interests.
Evaluate ISRO's role in advancing India's space capabilities and its contributions to global space exploration.
Analyze the various applications of remote sensing technology in sectors such as agriculture, urban planning, and disaster management.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the global space industry, including commercialization and international collaborations.
How does indigenous space technology contribute to India's self-reliance (Aatmanirbhar Bharat) vision?
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with the ISRO logo visible, against a backdrop of Earth.
GS3.trivia: India operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

GS3.headline: National Water Policy 2025 Framework Prioritizes Integrated Water Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti unveiled the draft framework for the National Water Policy 2025, focusing on integrated water resource management.
• The policy emphasizes a shift from a sectoral approach to a holistic, basin-level strategy, promoting inter-state cooperation.
• Key pillars include water conservation, efficient use, groundwater recharge, and sustainable management of both surface and groundwater resources.
• It advocates for the 'water-food-energy nexus' approach, recognizing the interdependence of these critical sectors.
• Public participation, data-driven decision making, and adoption of modern technologies are also central to the new policy's vision.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water Resources
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry for water resources in India.
Integrated Water Resource Management (IWRM) is a process which promotes the coordinated development and management of water, land and related resources.
National Water Policy (NWP) guides the planning and development of water resources.
Groundwater is a significant source of irrigation and drinking water in India.
The policy aims to address issues like water scarcity and pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Water Policy 2025 and its potential to address India's water crisis.
Discuss the challenges in implementing an integrated water resource management approach, particularly concerning inter-state water disputes and federalism.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous water policies in India and how the new framework aims to overcome their limitations.
Examine the importance of the 'water-food-energy nexus' in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
Suggest innovative solutions and community-based approaches for water conservation and efficient water use in both urban and rural areas.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a river flowing into a reservoir, with elements of agriculture and energy production, symbolizing integrated water management.
GS3.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

GS3.headline: Comprehensive Cybersecurity Policy 2025 Unveiled Amid Rising Digital Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025, replacing previous guidelines.
• The policy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and critical national infrastructure.
• Key focus areas include strengthening cyber defense capabilities, promoting indigenous cybersecurity products, and fostering international cooperation.
• It mandates robust security frameworks for government agencies and critical sectors, along with a focus on skill development and public awareness.
• The new framework addresses emerging threats like AI-powered cyberattacks, ransomware, and supply chain vulnerabilities.
gsSubTopics: Cyber Security
Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks
Role of Media and Social Networking Sites in Internal Security Challenges
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is the nodal ministry for cybersecurity.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to cyber incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy.
The policy aims to protect against cyberattacks, data breaches, and digital espionage.
India's first National Cyber Security Policy was released in 2013.
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025 and how it addresses contemporary cyber threats.
Analyze the challenges India faces in securing its cyberspace, considering its vast digital footprint and evolving threat landscape.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and collaboration in building a robust global cybersecurity framework.
Examine the interplay between cybersecurity, data privacy, and national security in the digital age.
Suggest measures to enhance public awareness and develop a skilled cybersecurity workforce in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A padlock icon superimposed on a digital circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity and protection.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amid Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was driven by persistent concerns over elevated inflation, despite recent moderation in some indicators.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target range of 2-6%.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while prioritizing price stability.
• Market analysts anticipate a possible rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflationary trends and robust economic data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Reverse Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of fostering economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation and ensuring financial stability in India.
Examine the factors contributing to persistent inflation in India and the government's role in addressing supply-side constraints.
How do global economic conditions and central bank actions in other major economies influence RBI's monetary policy decisions?
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing a stable repo rate line amidst fluctuating inflation indicators, symbolizing RBI's policy stance.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Project Great Indian Bustard Sees Boost with New Conservation Zones
GS3.explainer:
